query: Latest Russia Ukraine peace negotiations June 2026, any scheduled or held delegation talks late June or July 2026
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's the summary:

**Core answer:** As of June 29, 2026, **no in-person Russia-Ukraine delegation meeting is scheduled or held in the June 27 – July 9, 2026 window.** Direct talks remain suspended.

**Key findings:**
- Only **offers and readiness statements** exist — Turkey (FM Fidan, June 28) offered to host a new round; a Turkish source (June 29) said one could be arranged quickly *if* both sides signal readiness, confirming none is currently set.
- Putin (June 28) said Russia is ready but only on 2022 Istanbul terms, and is waiting for the US to return after the Iran crisis subsides. Ukraine's FM Sybiga (June 28) called the "Anchorage spirit" dead.
- The **last direct Ukraine-Russia meeting was February 2026 in the UAE**. No full delegation round has occurred in 2026 since then.
- **2026 cadence is effectively zero formal rounds** in the ~4 months before late June, versus three Istanbul rounds in 2025 (last on July 23, 2025).
- The only scheduled event in the window is a **NATO summit in Ankara (July 7-8)** — not a bilateral negotiation; Russia is not a party.
- The track is frozen due to US mediation attention being absorbed by the Iran war; a former Ukrainian FM predicts a real negotiation window may not open until autumn 2026.

sources.title: Putin reaffirms Russia's readiness for peace negotiations with Ukraine
sources.snippet: Putin (June 28, 2026): Russia ready for peace negotiations based on Istanbul agreements and Anchorage modalities; requires legally binding security guarantees.

sources.title: Source: Turkey can quickly organize Ukraine talks
sources.snippet: Turkish gov't source (June 29, 2026): organizing a new round would not take much time if Moscow and Kyiv signal readiness; references three 2025 Istanbul rounds.

sources.title: Turkey Offers to Host Ukraine-Russia Peace Talks
sources.snippet: Fidan (June 28, 2026) offered to bring delegations together again; NATO summit Ankara July 7-8; talks suspended.

sources.title: Turkey offers Ukraine and Russia to continue peace negotiations
sources.snippet: Turkey ready to convene RU/UA delegations again; NATO summit July 7-8 Ankara; draft declaration includes €70bn aid to Ukraine.

sources.title: Poland MFA: Zelenskyy and Putin should talk without intermediaries
sources.snippet: Sikorski (June 28, 2026): direct talks preferred; last direct RU-UA meeting was February 2026 in UAE trilateral summit.

sources.title: Sybiga declares the 'spirit of Anchorage' dead
sources.snippet: Sybiga (June 28, 2026): Anchorage spirit definitely dead; any plan without Ukraine doomed; Rubio June 25 confirmed no deal signed in Alaska 2025.

sources.title: Russia says waiting for US to resume peace negotiations
sources.snippet: Putin (June 28, 2026): waiting for US to resume talks after the intense phase of the Iranian dossier ends; diplomacy currently paralyzed.

sources.title: Prystayko interview on negotiation prospects
sources.snippet: Former Ukrainian FM Prystayko (June 29, 2026) predicts a window for real negotiations may open in autumn 2026.

sources.title: Ukraine, Russia hold third round of peace talks in Istanbul (July 23, 2025)
sources.snippet: Third round held July 23, 2025; meeting brief; agreed major prisoner exchange.

sources.title: Peace negotiations in the Russo-Ukrainian war (2022-present)
sources.snippet: Overview of rounds since 2022; renewed talks in 2025 under Trump; Russia refusing ceasefire calls.

sources.title: 2026 Iran war
sources.snippet: US/Israel-Iran war since Feb 28, 2026; ceasefire memorandum June 17; renewed US-Iran clashes June 26-28, 2026.

sources.title: Putin on why talks moved to Istanbul
sources.snippet: Putin recounts 2022 negotiation history; Istanbul March 29, 2022 draft terms.

key_facts: No in-person Russia-Ukraine delegation meeting is scheduled or held in the June 27 - July 9, 2026 window as of June 29, 2026; only mediation offers and readiness statements exist.
Direct peace negotiations remain suspended/paralyzed, largely frozen since the 2026 Iran war diverted US mediation capacity.
The last direct Ukraine-Russia representatives' meeting was in February 2026 in the UAE (US-mediated trilateral); no full delegation round has occurred in 2026 since then.
Putin (June 28, 2026) reaffirmed readiness to negotiate but only on the basis of the 2022 Istanbul agreements plus Anchorage modalities, awaiting the US to return after the Iran crisis subsides.
Ukrainian FM Sybiga (June 28, 2026) declared the 'spirit of Anchorage' dead and any plan without Ukraine 'unviable.'
Turkish FM Hakan Fidan (June 28, 2026) offered to host a new round; a Turkish source (June 29) said a round could be arranged quickly IF both sides signal readiness — none is currently arranged.
2025 saw three direct Istanbul rounds (May; third round July 23, 2025), each short and focused on prisoner exchanges; Aug 15, 2025 Anchorage summit produced no signed deal.
A NATO summit (not a Russia-Ukraine negotiation) is scheduled in Ankara July 7-8, 2026, with a draft declaration reportedly including €70bn aid to Ukraine.
Direct Ukraine-Russia contact continues only on prisoner/body exchanges, not political settlement.
Former Ukrainian FM Prystayko (June 29, 2026) predicted a real negotiation window may open only in autumn 2026.

data_points.metric: In-person Russia-Ukraine delegation meetings scheduled/held June 27 - July 9, 2026
Direct Ukraine-Russia delegation rounds held in 2026 (year-to-date)
Direct Istanbul negotiation rounds held in 2025
NATO summit in Ankara (not a bilateral RU-UA negotiation)
Proposed NATO/allied aid to Ukraine in draft Ankara summit declaration
